info.title: Triton Cloud Control Plane
info.description: Triton Cloud control-plane API. Phase 0 surface (`/v2/health`); subsequent phases add silos, instances, VPCs, audit, secrets, and the rest of DESIGN.md §14.

get.summary: List CNs with their managed-vs-legacy zone counts. Fleet-admin
get.description: only. Supports the operator workflow of "show me which CNs still have legacy zones I haven't adopted yet".

get.summary: List legacy VMs across the fleet, optionally filtered by host
get.description: CN. Fleet-admin only.

parameters.name: host_cn
parameters.description: Restrict to legacy VMs hosted on the given CN.

get.summary: Read a single legacy VM by SmartOS zone uuid, including full
get.description: NIC inventory. Fleet-admin only.

get.summary: Operator console for a discovered (non-tritond-managed) zone,
get.description: addressed by SmartOS zone uuid. Fleet-admin only — no tenant scoping. Serial only in practice for native zones; `kind=vnc` is honoured for bhyve / kvm zones. Not covered by the generated client.

get.summary: Materialise the Proteus per-port blueprint for a NIC. Auth:
get.description: requires a CN-bound API key with [`tritond_store::ApiKeyScope::Agent`]. The bound CN must have an in-progress claim for the port's instance.

post.summary: Atomically claim the next Pending provisioning job.
post.description:
Returns `200 OK` with `{"job": null}` when the queue is empty (the agent should sleep before its next poll), and `200 OK` with `{"job": {...}}` when a job was claimed and transitioned to `InProgress`. Auth: requires an API key with [`tritond_store::ApiKeyScope::Agent`].

The path is `/v2/agent/claim` rather than `/v2/agent/jobs/claim` because Dropshot's router cannot disambiguate a literal `claim` segment from a `{job_id}` path parameter at the same level.

post.summary: Report DHCP request activity the kmod observed for guest ports.
post.description: Auth: requires a CN-bound API key with [`tritond_store::ApiKeyScope::Agent`]. State-sample traffic — the response is always `200 OK`; items for unknown ports or ports with no lease record yet are silently skipped.

post.summary: Heartbeat from a bound agent. Lightweight ping — empty
post.description: body, just bumps `Cn.last_seen`. Auth: requires an API key with [`tritond_store::ApiKeyScope::Agent`] AND a `bound_to_cn` value (the per-CN keys minted at approval). Unbound Agent keys (legacy operator-minted) get 403 since there's no CN to attribute the heartbeat to.

get.summary: Materialise the full blueprint the agent needs to act on
get.description: a claimed job — instance + image + NICs + disks + authorised SSH public keys, all in one response. Auth: requires an API key with [`tritond_store::ApiKeyScope::Agent`].

post.summary: Mark a previously-claimed provisioning job terminal.
post.description: `outcome` is `Completed` for success or `Failed { reason }` for an agent-side abort. Auth: requires an API key with [`tritond_store::ApiKeyScope::Agent`].

post.summary: Ingest a batch of log lines from a registered agent.
post.description: Auth: requires an API key with [`tritond_store::ApiKeyScope::Agent`]. Batches larger than [`tritond_logs::LogBatch::MAX_LINES`] are rejected with `413 Payload Too Large`.

post.summary: Ingest a batch of metric samples from a registered agent.
post.description: Auth: requires an API key with [`tritond_store::ApiKeyScope::Agent`]. Batches larger than [`tritond_metrics::SampleBatch::MAX_SAMPLES`] are rejected with `413 Payload Too Large`.

post.summary: Report realized network state for a single resource. Auth:
post.description: requires a CN-bound API key with [`tritond_store::ApiKeyScope::Agent`]. Backward generation reports for the same `(resource, realizer)` are rejected with `409 Conflict`.

post.summary: Self-register a compute node. Anonymous endpoint (no API
post.description:
key needed) — the agent has none until approval completes. Tritond creates a [`CnState::Pending`] record with a fresh claim code unless the global auto-approve window is open, in which case the record is created directly Approved and the agent will retrieve its API key on the very next `/register/status` long-poll.

Idempotent on `server_uuid`: re-registration of a Pending record rotates the claim code; re-registration of an Approved record refreshes sysinfo without re-minting credentials.

get.summary: Long-poll for the per-CN API key. Anonymous endpoint
get.description:
authenticated only by holding the `poll_token` returned at registration. Tritond holds the connection open for up to ~30s waiting for state to flip from Pending to Approved (or for the auto-approve credential to be wired up); on timeout the agent re-polls.

The `api_key` field is populated **once** — on the first successful retrieval after approval. Subsequent calls return `state = Approved` with `api_key = None`.

post.summary: Full status sample from a bound agent. Replaces
post.description: `Cn.last_status` and bumps `last_seen`. Same auth shape as `agent_heartbeat`.

get.summary: Page through audit events. Returns at most `limit` events with
get.description: `seq > after_seq` plus the current chain head.

parameters.name: after_seq
parameters.description: Return events with `seq > after_seq`. Default 0 (start of chain).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum events to return. Default 100, max 1000.

get.summary: Fetch a single audit event by sequence number.
Walk the audit chain in `[from, to]` and recompute hashes.
get.description: Returns the first divergence (if any) plus the current head. Cheap on small ranges; auditors typically walk the entire chain once per export.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Last seq to walk. Default = current chain head.

get.summary: List the calling user's API keys. The plaintext secret is never
get.description: returned by this endpoint.

post.summary: Create a long-lived API key for the calling user. The plaintext
post.description: secret is included in the response **once** and never shown again.

delete.summary: Delete one of the calling user's API keys. Returns 404 if the
delete.description: id does not belong to the calling user.

get.summary: List the calling user's `User`-scoped images. Returns
get.description: only the caller's own images; the bound user_id is resolved from the authenticated principal.

post.summary: Register a `User`-scoped image owned by the caller.
Exchange username + password for an access/refresh token pair.
post.description: Returns 401 if credentials are invalid.

post.summary: Exchange a valid refresh token for a fresh access/refresh pair.
post.description: Returns 401 if the refresh token is invalid or expired.

get.summary: List the calling user's `User`-scoped SSH keys. Returns
get.description: only the caller's own keys; the bound user_id is resolved from the authenticated principal.

post.summary: Register a `User`-scoped SSH key owned by the caller.
Approve a Pending compute node by claim code. Mints the
post.description:
per-CN API key inside the same transaction that flips state; the plaintext is delivered to the agent via its long-poll on `/register/status`. Per-source-IP rate-limited.

Returns 404 for unknown / expired / already-approved codes (conflated to defeat enumeration). Returns 429 when the per-IP bucket is drained.

The path is `/v2/cn-approvals` rather than nested under `/v2/cns/...` because Dropshot's router cannot disambiguate a literal `approve` segment from the `{server_uuid}` parameter at the same level.

get.summary: Read the current auto-approve window, if open.
get.description:
Returns `200 OK` with `null` when no window is open.

Path lives at `/v2/cn-auto-approve` rather than nested under `/v2/cns/...` for the same reason as `/v2/cn-approvals`: a literal `auto-approve` segment cannot coexist with the `{server_uuid}` parameter on Dropshot's router.

post.summary: Open (or replace) the auto-approve window. While the
post.description: window is open, new self-registrations are promoted to Approved without operator action. Server-side cap: `duration_secs` is clamped to 24h.

delete.summary: Close the auto-approve window. Idempotent; no-op when no

get.summary: List compute nodes, optionally filtered by state. Operator
get.description: surface (root + future fleet-scoped operator role).

get.summary: Read a single compute-node record by `server_uuid`.

post.summary: Disable a compute node. Revokes the bound API key and
post.description: flips state to Disabled. The record is retained for audit visibility; a second call is idempotent.

get.summary: Range query for the per-CN metrics dashboard (NodeDetail
get.description:
page's Metrics tab). Returns one series per CPU mode for the requested CN's global zone -- host services + kernel time not charged to a tenant zone. Mirrors the legacy cmon `/v1/discover` -> per-CN scrape pattern, just typed.

Auth: requires fleet-read access to the CN (same scope as `get_cn`). No tenant filter -- per-CN samples are operator surface.

post.summary: Set the placement role for a compute node. Operator-only.

get.summary: List every cluster-wide configuration key with its current
get.description: value, built-in default, description, restart requirement, and whether an environment variable is currently overriding it at boot. Fleet-admin only.

get.summary: Read one configuration key. Returns `404` for an unknown key.
get.description: Fleet-admin only.

put.summary: Set one configuration key. Returns `404` for an unknown key,
put.description: `400` for an ill-typed value, the updated entry otherwise. The new value is persisted to FoundationDB and takes effect on the next `tritond` restart. Fleet-admin only; the change is recorded in the audit log.

delete.summary: Reset one configuration key to its built-in default. Returns
delete.description: `404` for an unknown key, the (default-valued) entry otherwise. Persisted to FoundationDB; takes effect on the next restart. Fleet-admin only; recorded in the audit log.

get.summary: Liveness check. Returns service status and version string.
List Public images. Anonymous-accessible — Public means
get.description: public, so unauthenticated probes get the catalog.

post.summary: Create a `Public` image. Root-only via Cedar.
post.description: Returns 400 if `sha256` is not 64 lowercase hex chars or if `size_bytes` is zero. Returns 409 if the name is already in use among Public images.

get.summary: Read a single image by id. Returns 404 when the image
get.description: does not exist OR when the principal cannot see it (cross-scope visibility deny).

delete.summary: Delete an image by id. Returns 404 when the image does
delete.description: not exist OR the principal lacks ownership for the image's scope: * `Public` — root only. * `Silo` / `Tenant` / `Project` — any tenant member of the resolved tenant (Phase 0 = same-tenant access). * `User` — only the owning user (or root).

get.summary: List every metadata entry stored at one scope. RBAC: silo-member
get.description: for `scope=silo`; tenant-member (of the scope's owning tenant) for `scope=tenant|project|instance`.

get.summary: Read one metadata entry by key (query parameter). The key may
get.description: contain `/` (`config/ntp-servers`, `state/active-color`, …), hence the query-string placement.

put.summary: Upsert one metadata entry. Returns the stored entry plus the
put.description: scope's new generation counter (the realized-view cache key). Validation per `tritond_store::validate_meta_entry`: namespace + scope + value-type + byte-cap rules; `guest_writable=true` is only accepted on `guest/*` keys at instance scope.

delete.summary: Delete one metadata entry. 404 if the key is absent (no

get.summary: List every silo, sorted by `name`. Phase 0 has no per-operator
get.description: silo visibility filter so this returns the full set.

post.summary: Create a silo. Returns 201 with the created silo.
post.description: Fails with 409 if a silo with the requested name already exists.

get.summary: Look up a silo by id. Returns 404 if no such silo exists.

post.summary: Register an image from a tritond image bundle (silo-scoped
post.description:
only). tritond fetches the bundle once at registration, validates the manifest, re-hashes the content, and populates every Image-record field from the manifest. The returned `Image` carries `compatibility = Some(...)` so the per-CN agent enforces brand + min_smartos_platform gates at provision time. Returns 400 on a malformed bundle or sha256 mismatch, 502 if `bundle_url` is unreachable, 409 on a name or content collision within the silo.

The path is `/v2/silos/{silo_id}/image-bundles` rather than `/v2/silos/{silo_id}/images/from-bundle` because Dropshot's router cannot disambiguate a literal `from-bundle` segment from a sibling `{image_id}` parameter at the same level.

get.summary: List the images whose scope is exactly `Silo { silo_id }`
get.description: (does NOT include Public — use `/v2/tenants/{tenant_id}/images` for the unioned tenant view).

post.summary: Register a `Silo`-scoped image.

get.summary: List the SSH keys whose scope is exactly `Silo { silo_id }`
get.description: (does NOT include Public — use `/v2/tenants/{tenant_id}/ssh-keys` for the unioned tenant view).

post.summary: Register a `Silo`-scoped SSH key. The server parses
post.description: `public_key` as openssh format and computes the SHA-256 fingerprint. Returns 400 if the key cannot be parsed, 409 if the name or fingerprint is already in use within the silo.

get.summary: List the tenants in a silo. Operator-facing surface; root
get.description: can administer tenants directly.

post.summary: Create a tenant in a silo. Returns 409 if the tenant name is
post.description: already in use within the silo, 404 if the silo does not exist.

get.summary: Read a single tenant. Returns 404 when the tenant does not
get.description: exist or belongs to a different silo (cross-silo probes do not learn that the resource exists).

delete.summary: Delete a tenant. Returns 404 when the tenant does not exist
delete.description: or belongs to a different silo. Phase-0 deletion is permissive (does not check for child projects); the block-on-children guard belongs in a future cleanup.

get.summary: List Public SSH keys. Anonymous-accessible — Public means
get.description: public, so unauthenticated probes get the catalog.

post.summary: Create a `Public` SSH key. Root-only via Cedar.
post.description: Returns 400 if the key cannot be parsed as openssh, 409 if the name or fingerprint is already in use among Public keys.

get.summary: Read a single SSH key by id. Returns 404 when the key
get.description: does not exist OR when the principal cannot see it (cross-scope visibility deny).

delete.summary: Delete an SSH key by id. Returns 404 when the key does
delete.description: not exist OR the principal lacks ownership for the key's scope: * `Public` — root only. * `Silo` / `Tenant` / `Project` — any tenant member of the resolved tenant (Phase 0 = same-tenant access). * `User` — only the owning user (or root).

get.summary: List every registered manta-storage cluster, sorted by name.
get.description: Operator surface (root-only via Cedar root-allows-all).

post.summary: Register a new storage cluster (mantad / mantafs / manta-block).
post.description: The bearer token submitted in the body is held server-side and never returned by any GET — see [`StorageClusterView`] for the redacted wire shape.

get.summary: Read a single registered storage cluster by id.

delete.summary: Drop a storage cluster registration. Idempotent.
`DELETE /admin/v1/access-keys/{access_key_id}`. Note that the
delete.description: AKID alone identifies the key — there's no per-user nesting here, mirroring mantad's surface.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/buckets` — all buckets in the cluster.
get.description: `?stats=true` forwards `?stats=1` to mantad and includes per-bucket object counts + total bytes (a more expensive scan).

post.summary: `POST /admin/v1/buckets` — create a bucket.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}` — bucket detail.

delete.summary: `DELETE /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}` — delete an empty bucket.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/buckets/{bucket}/objects` — paged object listing.
`GET /admin/v1/cluster` — cluster summary.

post.summary: Trigger an out-of-band health probe against the cluster's
post.description: `/admin/v1/cluster` summary, persist the observed status to `last_observed_at`, and return the refreshed view. POST because it actively mutates state.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/membership` — current membership view.
`GET /admin/v1/nodes` — list all nodes.

post.summary: `POST /admin/v1/nodes` — register a peer.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/nodes/{node_id}` — single-node detail.

parameters.name: node_id
parameters.description: Numeric storage-node identifier (mirrors `manta_place::NodeId`, which is a `u32`). Re-serialised verbatim to mantad.

delete.summary: `DELETE /admin/v1/nodes/{node_id}` — drop a peer.

parameters.name: node_id
parameters.description: Numeric storage-node identifier (mirrors `manta_place::NodeId`, which is a `u32`). Re-serialised verbatim to mantad.

post.summary: `POST /admin/v1/nodes/{node_id}/drain`.

parameters.name: node_id
parameters.description: Numeric storage-node identifier (mirrors `manta_place::NodeId`, which is a `u32`). Re-serialised verbatim to mantad.

post.summary: `POST /admin/v1/nodes/{node_id}/reweight`.

parameters.name: node_id
parameters.description: Numeric storage-node identifier (mirrors `manta_place::NodeId`, which is a `u32`). Re-serialised verbatim to mantad.

post.summary: `POST /admin/v1/nodes/{node_id}/undrain`.

parameters.name: node_id
parameters.description: Numeric storage-node identifier (mirrors `manta_place::NodeId`, which is a `u32`). Re-serialised verbatim to mantad.

post.summary: Configure (or rotate) the per-cluster presigner identity.
post.description: `s3_endpoint` updates the cluster's data-plane URL; pass `None` to leave it unchanged. Empty `access_key_id` + `secret_access_key` clear the presigner. Mismatched (one empty, one set) → 409.

post.summary: Mint a single-shot presigned GET URL (downloads or shareable
post.description: links).

post.summary: Mint a single-shot presigned PUT URL (uploads < 5 MB).
post.description: Returns 409 when the cluster has no presigner configured.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/users`.

post.summary: `POST /admin/v1/users`.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/users/{user}`.

delete.summary: `DELETE /admin/v1/users/{user}`.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/users/{user}/access-keys`.

post.summary: `POST /admin/v1/users/{user}/access-keys` — secret returned
post.description: once on the response. Caller must capture it; mantad does not retain the cleartext.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/users/{user}/policies` — list attached policy
get.description: names. Returns a Vec<String> verbatim from mantad.

get.summary: `GET /admin/v1/users/{user}/policies/{policy}` — raw policy JSON.

put.description: policy doc. Body is the raw policy document; mantad does not validate schema beyond "is JSON".

delete.summary: `DELETE /admin/v1/users/{user}/policies/{policy}`.

get.summary: Read the OIDC IdP config for a tenant. The client secret is
get.description: never returned. 404 when no IdP is configured.

post.summary: Configure the OIDC identity provider for a tenant. Returns
post.description: 502 if the discovery document cannot be fetched, 404 if the tenant does not exist, 409 if a different tenant already claims the same `issuer_url`, otherwise 201 with the redacted view of what was persisted.

delete.summary: Remove the OIDC IdP config for a tenant. Federated users
delete.description: in that tenant will fail to authenticate until a new config is posted.

get.summary: List images visible to the tenant: Public + Silo (of
get.description: tenant's silo) + Tenant.

post.summary: Register a `Tenant`-scoped image.

get.summary: List the projects inside a tenant.

post.summary: Create a project in a tenant. Returns 409 if the project
post.description: name is already in use within that tenant.

get.summary: Read a single project. Returns 404 when the project does not
get.description: exist or belongs to a different tenant (cross-tenant probes do not learn that other tenants exist).

delete.summary: Delete a project. Returns 404 when the project does not exist
delete.description: or belongs to a different tenant.

get.summary: List FloatingIps owned by a project.

post.summary: Allocate a FloatingIp from the requested family's pool.
post.description: Returns 409 if the name is already in use within the project. Returns 404 if the project does not exist or belongs to a different tenant. The returned FloatingIp starts unattached.

get.summary: Read a single FloatingIp. Returns 404 if the FloatingIp
get.description: does not exist or belongs to a different tenant or project.

delete.summary: Release a FloatingIp back to its pool. Returns 409 if the
delete.description: FloatingIp is currently attached (operator must detach first).

post.summary: Atomically attach a FloatingIp to a NIC, replacing any
post.description: existing attachment. The target NIC must live in the same tenant + project as the FloatingIp; mismatch surfaces as 404.

post.summary: Detach a FloatingIp from its current NIC. Idempotent — a
post.description: detach on an already-detached FloatingIp is a no-op that returns the current record.

get.summary: List images visible to the project: Public + Silo (of
get.description: project's silo) + Tenant (of project's tenant) + Project. This is the practical "what can a project member launch from?" query.

post.summary: Register a `Project`-scoped image.

get.summary: List instances in a project.

post.summary: Create an instance in a project.
post.description:
Returns 400 if `cpu == 0` or `memory_bytes == 0`. Returns 404 if any referenced resource (image, subnet, ssh-keys) does not exist or lives outside this tenant/project (or, for image/ssh-key which remain silo-scoped in E-3, outside the tenant's silo). Returns 409 if the instance name is already taken in the project.

Phase 0 ships synchronous lifecycle: the create handler transitions the new instance through Pending → Running before responding. A future slice introduces an async provisioning queue + stub executor; the API surface stays the same but tests will observe Pending-then-Running transitions instead of an instant Running.

get.summary: Read a single instance.

delete.summary: Delete an instance. Returns 409 if the instance is not in
delete.description: a deletable state (must be Stopped or Failed); pass `?force=true` to override and delete from any state. Returns 404 if the instance does not exist or belongs to a different tenant or project. The tritond record is cleared synchronously; the agent vmadm-deletes the SmartOS zone asynchronously via a `JobKind::Delete` job.

get.summary: Browser-facing serial / VNC console for a managed instance.
get.description: Authorises via the `instance_console` Cedar action; only valid while the instance is Running. `?kind=vnc` is refused for brands without a framebuffer (anything but `bhyve` / `kvm`). Not covered by the generated client (`#[channel]` endpoints are consumed by the hand-rolled admin-backend proxy).

get.summary: List the Disks attached to an instance. Phase 0 produces
get.description: exactly one (the auto-created `"boot"`); future multi-disk attach lands as a follow-on slice.

get.summary: Read a single Disk. Returns 404 if the Disk does not exist
get.description: or belongs to a different tenant, project, or instance.

get.summary: Tail the most recent log lines for one source on one VM.
get.description:
Pagination via `before_seq` -- pass the smallest `seq` from the previous response to fetch older lines.

Auth: requires a tenant-scoped credential with read access to the named instance (same envelope as `get_project_instance`).

get.summary: Range query for the per-instance metrics chart on the VM
get.description:
detail view. Returns one numeric series per distinct `series` identity field encountered in the requested window. Counter datums are emitted as per-bucket deltas; gauges as last-value per bucket.

Auth: requires a tenant-scoped credential with read access to the named instance.

get.summary: List the NICs attached to an instance. Phase 0 produces
get.description: exactly one (the auto-created `"primary"`); a future slice adds NIC attach/detach.

get.summary: Read a single NIC. Returns 404 if the NIC does not exist or
get.description: belongs to a different tenant, project, or instance.

post.summary: Restart a running instance. Returns 409 if the instance is
post.description: not in `Running` state.

post.summary: Start a stopped instance. Returns 409 if the instance is
post.description: not in `Stopped` state.

post.summary: Stop a running instance. Returns 409 if the instance is
post.description: not in `Running` state.

get.summary: Read a project's quota. Returns 404 when the project does
get.description: not exist, lives in a different tenant, or has no quota set (no record means "unlimited").

put.summary: Set (or replace) the resource quota on a project. Returns
put.description: 404 when the project does not exist or belongs to a different tenant. The server assigns `updated_at`. Quotas are not enforced in Phase 0; the record is stored for the eventual instance-create flow to consult.

delete.summary: Remove a project's quota (project becomes unlimited).
delete.description: Returns 404 when the project does not exist, lives in a different tenant, or had no quota set.

get.summary: List SSH keys visible to the project: Public + Silo (of
get.description: project's silo) + Tenant (of project's tenant) + Project.

post.summary: Register a `Project`-scoped SSH key.

get.summary: List the VPCs inside a project. Returns 404 when the tenant or
get.description: project does not exist (or the project belongs to a different tenant).

post.summary: Create a VPC in a project. Returns 400 if neither `ipv4_block`
post.description: nor `ipv6_block` is provided. Returns 409 if a VPC with the same name already exists in the project. The server assigns `id`, `vni` (random in `[4096, 2^24)`, unique rack-wide), and `created_at`.

get.summary: Read a single VPC. Returns 404 when the VPC does not exist or
get.description: belongs to a different tenant or project (cross-tenant probes do not learn that the resource exists).

delete.summary: Delete a VPC. Returns 404 when the VPC does not exist or
delete.description: belongs to a different tenant or project. Returns 409 if the VPC still has subnets attached — operators must clear subnets before deleting the parent VPC (Phase 0 has no cascade).

get.summary: List active DHCP leases for a VPC. Each entry was written when
get.description: tritond pre-assigned an IP to a NIC at instance create.

get.summary: Look up a lease by MAC.

delete.summary: Operator-driven release: remove the lease record. The
delete.description: underlying IP is freed for re-allocation; sticky-by-MAC for this MAC is broken until the operator re-creates the reservation.

get.summary: Read the per-VPC DHCP pool config. Returns the body wrapped
get.description: `Some` when set; returns `None` when the operator hasn't customised this VPC.

put.summary: Create or replace the per-VPC DHCP pool config.

delete.summary: Remove the per-VPC DHCP pool config (revert to defaults).

get.summary: List DHCP reservations (operator-pinned MAC→IP mappings) in a VPC.

post.summary: Create a DHCP reservation pinning a MAC to a specific IPv4.
post.description: Returns 409 if the MAC is already reserved with a different IP.

get.summary: Look up a reservation by MAC.

delete.summary: Remove a reservation by MAC.

get.summary: List firewall rules scoped to a VPC, sorted by `priority`
get.description: descending (highest evaluates first).

post.summary: Create a firewall rule scoped to a VPC. Slice 1: every NIC in
post.description: the VPC inherits every rule (no security-group attachment yet). The server assigns `id` and `created_at`.

delete.summary: Delete a firewall rule by id.

get.summary: List NAT gateways inside a VPC.

post.summary: Create a NAT gateway in a VPC. Returns 409 if the name is
post.description: already in use within the VPC. The returned record includes the reserved public source address.

get.summary: Read a single NAT gateway.

delete.summary: Delete a NAT gateway and release its public address.

get.summary: List route tables inside a VPC.

post.summary: Create a non-main route table in a VPC. Returns 409 if the
post.description: name is already in use within the VPC.

get.summary: Read a single route table.

delete.summary: Delete a non-main route table. Returns 409 while subnets or

get.summary: List routes inside a route table.

post.summary: Create a route in a route table. Returns 409 if the
post.description: destination is already in use in the table.

get.summary: Read a single route.

delete.summary: Delete a route.

get.summary: List the subnets inside a VPC. Returns 404 when the tenant,
get.description: project, or VPC does not exist (or is in the wrong parent).

post.summary: Create a subnet in a VPC. Returns 400 if neither
post.description: `ipv4_block` nor `ipv6_block` is provided. Returns 409 if a subnet with the same name already exists in the VPC, if a CIDR is not contained in the parent VPC's matching-family CIDR, or if a CIDR overlaps an existing subnet's CIDR. The server assigns `id` and `created_at`.

get.summary: Read a single subnet. Returns 404 when the subnet does not
get.description: exist or belongs to a different tenant, project, or VPC.

delete.summary: Delete a subnet. Returns 404 when the subnet does not exist
delete.description: or belongs to a different tenant, project, or VPC.

get.summary: List SSH keys visible to the tenant: Public + Silo (of
get.description: tenant's silo) + Tenant.

post.summary: Register a `Tenant`-scoped SSH key.

oneOf.description: Authenticated operator with an `is_root` flag captured at the time of the event.
Authenticated via API key. `key_id` lets auditors trace the credential without revealing it.
No credential presented. Used only for events where logging the unauthenticated principal is intentional (e.g. login attempts; not for routine deny-on-anonymous probes).
Internal system action (bootstrap, cron, replication).

AddressFamily.description: Address family selector used by [`NewFloatingIp`] to ask the server to allocate from one or the other Phase 0 pool.

AdoptableState.description: Whether a [`LegacyVm`] is eligible for adoption into a tritond tenant/project. Phase B leaves every legacy VM as `Unevaluated`; the (deferred) Phase D adoption flow promotes to `Yes` after brand + NIC compatibility checks, or `No(reason)` when the zone can't be rewritten onto the proteus dataplane.

oneOf.description: Adoption has not yet been evaluated for this zone.
Zone can be adopted: brand + NIC layout are compatible with a tritond VPC + proteus port.
Zone cannot be adopted; reason is operator-readable.

AgentPortBlueprint.description:
Opaque Proteus port blueprint returned to a bound CN agent.

`blueprint_postcard_base64` is a base64-encoded `proteus_api::blueprint::PortBlueprint`. Tritond keeps it opaque at the public API boundary so the agent can decode it against the same Proteus userspace client version it will apply locally.

AgentStatusRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v2/agent/status`.

`payload` is opaque to tritond — agents pick the shape — but the Triton-classic shape is `{ vms, zpools, meminfo, diskinfo, boot_time, timestamp }`. Stored verbatim on the `Cn` record's `last_status` field; surfaced via `tcadm cn show`.

ApiKeyCreated.description:
Response body for `POST /v2/auth/api-keys`.

`secret` is the wire-form key. It is shown to the operator **once**; the server retains only a bcrypt hash.

ApiKeyScope.description:
Permission scope attached to an [`ApiKey`]. Determines which Cedar actions the key may authorise *beyond* what its owning user could do natively. The scope check runs at the auth layer, before Cedar — see `services/tritond/src/auth.rs`. Mapping from scope to allowed actions is exhaustive there so the compiler flags any new [`crate::types`] action that hasn't been classified.

Records persisted before this field existed deserialise as [`ApiKeyScope::Full`] thanks to `#[serde(default)]` on [`ApiKey::scope`]; this preserves the pre-scope behaviour where every minted key had the full permissions of the owning user.

oneOf.description: Full access — equivalent to authenticating as the owning user. Default when no scope is specified.
List/get on every resource, plus audit chain reads. Cannot mutate state, mint or delete API keys, or change IdP config. Useful for monitoring agents and read replicas.
Audit chain reads only (`audit_list`, `audit_fetch`, `audit_verify`). Useful for compliance pipelines that should see "who did what when" but never the resources themselves.

Provisioning-agent scope: `agent_claim` and `agent_complete` only. A key with this scope cannot read tenant resources or audit events; it can only pull jobs from the queue and report outcomes. Used by the per-CN `tritonagent` to authenticate to tritond's `/v2/agent/*` surface.

ApiKeyView.description: Wire-safe view of an [`ApiKey`]: identifying metadata, no hash.

ApproveCnRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v2/cns/approve`.

The operator presents the claim code displayed on the CN's console (or syslog, or the `/var/lib/tritonagent/claim-code` file). Hyphens and case are normalized server-side.

AttachFloatingIpRequest.description: Body for `POST /attach`. Names the target NIC the FloatingIp should swap onto. The server resolves silo + project + instance from the NIC; cross-tenant targets surface as 404.

AuditEvent.description: Materialised audit event. The `hash` is `SHA-256(canonical-JSON of every field except hash itself)` so a verifier reading any prefix of the chain can recompute and compare.

AutoApproveWindow.description: Auto-approve window: while open, new Pending CN registrations are promoted to Approved without operator action. Bounded by both wall time (`expires_at`) and a remaining-count budget so an operator can't accidentally leave the window open forever.

opened_by.description: Operator who opened the window (free-text, captured from the authenticated principal at open time).

remaining_count.description: Remaining auto-approvals before the window closes. `None` means "unlimited count, time-bound only".

ChainHead.description: Snapshot of where the chain currently ends.

hash.description: Its `hash` — the value the next append will use as `prev_hash`.

seq.description: Sequence number of the most recently appended event.

ClaimJobRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v2/agent/jobs/claim`.

`claimed_by` is the agent's own identity — used by the store as the [`ProvisioningJob::claimed_by`] field, and rolled into audit events so concurrent agents can be told apart.

ClaimJobResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /v2/agent/jobs/claim`.

`job` is `Some(...)` when the queue had a Pending job and the claim succeeded; `None` when the queue is empty. The HTTP status is always `200 OK`; the agent reads the `job` field to decide whether to do work or sleep until the next poll.

CnRole.description:
Placement role for a compute node.

M1 starts every CN as tenant-capable. Operators can mark a CN as edge-only, or both tenant and edge, before the edge placer starts assigning firehyve/fhrun north/south instances.

oneOf.description: Eligible for tenant workload placement.
Eligible for north/south edge placement.
Eligible for tenant workload and north/south edge placement.

CnState.description: Lifecycle state of a compute-node registration.

oneOf.description: Self-registered; awaiting operator approval (or auto-approve window). Carries an active `claim_code` until approval or expiry.
Approved and active. The per-CN API key bound via [`ApiKey::bound_to_cn`] is the agent's credential for the rest of the `/v2/agent/*` surface.

Explicitly disabled by an operator. The bound API key is revoked and the record stays for audit visibility. A fresh registration from the same `server_uuid` re-arms the record back to `Pending` (awaiting approval) — i.e. "re-enable with fresh credentials"; the disable event remains in the audit chain.

CnView.description: Wire-safe view of a [`Cn`]: strips the transient plaintext credential field and any other secrets from operator-facing JSON.

claim_code.description: Displayed in the `XXX-XXX` format for operator readability. `None` once approved or expired.

last_status.description: Last full status payload published by the agent (vms / zpools / meminfo / disk_usage / boot_time blob).

ConfigEntry.description: One cluster-wide configuration key with its current value, default, description, and operational metadata. Returned by the `/v2/config` endpoints; consumed by `tcadm config` and the admin console.

env_override.description: Environment variable currently shadowing this key at boot (`env > FDB > default`), if any.

restart_required.description: Whether changing this key requires a `tritond` restart to take effect. Currently `true` for every key.

value.description: Value as stored in FoundationDB (the built-in default when no value has been written). If `env_override` is set, the daemon is actually running with the environment variable's value, not this one.

Datum.description: Single-value datum. Schemas state which variant they emit; storage backends route each variant to the right physical table.

oneOf.description: Monotonic counter. Range queries return per-bucket deltas.
Instantaneous gauge.
Instantaneous gauge, integer-typed.

Decision.description: Cedar decision recorded with the event.

oneOf.description: Cedar permitted the action.
Cedar denied the action.
Decision was not evaluated (e.g. recorded before Cedar runs).

DhcpLease.description: One lease the IPAM has handed out. Written when tritond pre-assigns an IP (γ.4 hook on `create_instance`); updated by the lease-renewal event consumer (δ slice, not yet wired).

instance_id.description: The instance the lease was assigned to at create time. Stays pinned through instance delete so sticky-by-MAC keeps working when the operator re-creates with the same MAC; cleared when the operator explicitly releases the lease.

last_msg_type.description: Last DHCP message type observed by the kmod for this MAC (DISCOVER, REQUEST, RELEASE, …). `None` until tritonagent starts forwarding events (δ slice).

nic_id.description: NIC the lease is bound to.

DhcpLeaseActivity.description: One DHCP message the kmod observed for a guest port, forwarded by the bound CN agent draining the Proteus event ring. Tritond uses these to keep the lease record's `last_renewed_at` fresh so the reconciler's idle-GC heuristic doesn't mistake a long-lived VM for an orphaned lease. RELEASE / DECLINE are recorded as the last message type but never expire the lease (persistent-lease policy); only DISCOVER / REQUEST advance the renewal clock.

client_mac.description: Client MAC as the kmod saw it, canonical lowercase colon-separated form (e.g. `02:08:20:ab:cd:ef`). Used for a cross-check against the stored NIC; not the lookup key.

msg_type.description: DHCP message type (RFC 2132 option 53): 1 DISCOVER, 2 OFFER, 3 REQUEST, 4 DECLINE, 5 ACK, 6 NAK, 7 RELEASE, 8 INFORM.

port_id.description: Proteus port id — equal to the NIC id.

xid.description: BOOTP transaction id from the request.

DhcpLeaseActivityReport.description: Request body for `POST /v2/agent/dhcp-lease-activity`. A batch — the agent drains the event ring on each poll and forwards every DHCP request it found in one round-trip.

DhcpOptionRaw.description: Wire shape of one raw DHCP option. Matches proteus's `DhcpOption`.

value.description: Up to 250 bytes (option-length byte caps at 255 minus 2 for code+length, conservatively 250). Serde encodes as a JSON array of integers; the wire-cap check lives in the create handler, not here.

DhcpPool.description: Per-VPC DHCPv4 pool config + segment-wide DHCP options. Singleton per VPC; absence means "use the subnet CIDR directly with defaults."

additional_options.description: VPC-wide DHCP options merged into every per-port response (after `Dhcpv4Options.additional_options` from the port's own blueprint). Example: an NTP server, a vendor-specific PXE pointer, etc.

excluded_ipv4.description: Operator-specified IPv4 addresses to skip during allocation. Useful for reserving the gateway, broadcast, or external services living on the subnet.

lease_seconds_default.description: Renewal-cadence hint emitted in DHCP option 51.

DhcpReservation.description:
Operator-pinned MAC→IP mapping. Survives instance delete; an instance booting later with a matching MAC reuses this IP.

Per-MAC additional options are merged on top of the per-VPC pool's `additional_options` and the per-port blueprint's options at response synthesis time.

Disk.description:
Per-instance persistent storage. Phase 0 auto-creates a single boot disk per instance, sized from the source image and tagged with that image's id. Bhyve images are clamped to the M1 default VM boot-disk floor until packages/SKUs expose an explicit size. The disk record is the metadata view; the actual content (zfs dataset, mantafs object, etc.) is materialized by the agent at provisioning time.

Multi-disk attach (data disks beyond boot) lands as a follow-on slice. The wire shape here is stable across that change — a `kind: Data` disk is a strict superset of what `Boot` carries once `source_image_id` is allowed to be `None`.

size_bytes.description: Total size in bytes. Boot disks default via [`default_boot_disk_size_bytes`]; future data disks accept an explicit operator-supplied size.

source_image_id.description: Image the boot disk was sourced from. `Some` for `Boot` disks that came from a registered image; `None` for blank `Data` disks (Phase 0 has no callers that produce `None`, but the type allows it for the multi-disk slice).

DiskKind.description: What a disk is for. Phase 0 ships only `Boot` disks (auto-created from the instance's image at instance create time). `Data` is reserved for the future multi-disk attach slice.

oneOf.description: Bootable; sourced from an Image. The instance boots from this disk on first start.
Blank persistent storage attached to the instance. Reserved for the future multi-disk slice; not yet exercised.

FirewallIcmpFilter.description: ICMP type + code pair carried as a struct (instead of `(u8, u8)`) so the JSON schema is OpenAPI v3.0–compatible (tuple arrays are not representable until v3.1).

FirewallRule.description: Per-VPC firewall rule. Slice 1 ships a flat per-VPC list — every NIC in the VPC inherits every rule (no security-group attachment yet). Tritond translates this directly into proteus `triton_vpc::tritond_intent_v1::FirewallRuleIntentV1` when computing the per-port blueprint, which the dataplane compiles into one `SecurityGroupRule` per record.

icmp_type_code.description: Optional ICMP type/code filter. Only valid when `protocol` is `Icmp4` or `Icmp6`; the API rejects mismatches at create time.

priority.description: Higher numbers evaluate first inside a layer.

source_ports.description: Inclusive port range. `None` ⇒ any port. Ignored for non-TCP/UDP.

FloatingIp.description:
Tenant-managed external IP, allocated from a fleet pool and attachable to any NIC in the same project. Persists independent of any single instance: when the attached instance is deleted, the `attached_to` field clears but the FloatingIp itself stays owned by the project and reusable.

Each FloatingIp represents *one* address — the wire shape does not bifurcate IPv4 and IPv6. The address family is implicit in the `address` bits.

Phase 0 invariants vs other clouds:

* **Project-owned, not instance-owned.** Instance delete → auto-detach, never auto-release. (AWS detaches but leaves the IP at the account level, which loses the project-scoping story; we keep the project envelope.) * **Symmetric IPv4 + IPv6.** No "v6 floating IPs are a separate type" wart. * **Atomic attach replaces.** A second attach with a different NIC swaps the binding in one transaction; no detach-then-attach window in the control plane. * **Delete is explicit.** Detaching does not auto-release; the FloatingIp persists and is visible in `tcadm` listings until the operator deletes it.

address.description: The actual external address. Allocated at create time from the requested family's pool. Immutable for the life of the record.

attached_to.description: Currently-bound NIC, or `None` if floating. Replaced atomically by `attach`; cleared by `detach` and by the instance-delete cascade.

FloatingIpAttachment.description: Where a [`FloatingIp`] is currently attached. `None` (i.e. the `FloatingIp::attached_to` field is `None`) means the IP is allocated to the project but not bound to any NIC — it persists across attach/detach cycles and across instance deletes.

IdpConfigView.description: Wire-safe view of an [`IdpConfig`] with the client secret redacted. Returned by `GET /v2/silos/{silo_id}/idp`.

Image.description: Image catalog entry. Multi-scope as of Slice F: see [`ImageScope`] for the variants. Phase 0 ships only the metadata record — image content lives in mantafs / object storage and is not modelled here. Operators register images by URL + sha256 and trust the caller for the content match; an eventual import pipeline will pre-stage content in storage and verify the digest before the record is persisted.

compatibility.description: Optional host-compatibility constraints, populated when the image was registered via the bundle path (image-create with `bundle_url`). When `Some`, the per-CN agent rejects a Provision before vmadm if the instance brand or host platform fails the constraints. `None` skips the gate (legacy / explicit-fields image-create path); a future slice migrates every Image record to carry compatibility metadata.

os.description: OS family identifier (e.g. `linux`, `windows`, `smartos`). Stringly-typed in Phase 0; will tighten to an enum once the instance brand model lands.

scope.description: Visibility scope. The variant carries the parent identity (silo_id / tenant_id / project_id / user_id) for the non-Public scopes; visibility checks resolve up the project → tenant → silo chain when needed.

sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the image content. Server-validated for length (64 chars) and charset at create time.

source_url.description: Optional URL where the image content can be fetched. `None` means the content is registered out-of-band (e.g. already in mantafs at a known path resolved by image_id).

version.description: OS version / distro tag (e.g. `ubuntu-22.04`, `windows-server-2022`). Free-form for Phase 0.

ImageCompatibility.description: Host-compatibility constraints on an [`Image`]. Mirrors `tritond_image_manifest::Compatibility` exactly — the bundle ingest path copies the manifest's compatibility block in without translation. The per-CN agent enforces these gates at provision time.

brand.description: SmartOS brand the image is built for (e.g. `joyent-minimal`, `lx`). Compared against the instance's requested brand.

min_smartos_platform.description: SmartOS platform buildstamp (`YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ`); the host's platform buildstamp must be lexicographically `>=` this value. `None` means "any platform."

ImageScope.description:
Visibility scope of an [`Image`]. The variant determines who can see and use the image: `Public` is everyone (including anonymous probes on the public listing endpoint); `Silo` is every member of any tenant under that silo; `Tenant` is every member of that tenant; `Project` is every tenant member with project access (Phase 0: every tenant member); `User` is one specific user.

The variant carries everything the visibility predicate needs — there are no denormalised silo_id / tenant_id fields on `Image`. For `Project`, the resolver looks up the project to derive its tenant + silo when needed; for `Tenant`, the resolver looks up the tenant for its silo when needed. Cold path; correctness > one extra read.

Instance.description:
Tenant compute instance. Project-scoped; references one image (boot media), one subnet (network attach point), and zero-or-more SSH keys (injected into authorized_keys at provisioning time).

Phase 0 ships only the metadata + lifecycle state machine. The actual provisioning is faked synchronously inside the create handler; a future slice introduces the intent queue and stub executor that will become the swap-out point for a real `tritonagent`.

Several fields that real cloud instances carry are deliberately omitted in v0: cloud-init userdata, tags/labels, affinity rules, console URL, migration history. Each will land as the consuming use case ships. The hosting CN is recorded once placement lands so subsequent lifecycle jobs return to the same SmartOS host.

brand.description: SmartOS brand this instance's host-side VM runs as. Derived from the boot image's compatibility block at create time; `NotApplicable` for older records and explicit-fields images.

host_cn_uuid.description:
SmartOS compute node that owns this instance's host-side VM.

`None` is retained for in-process test/stub jobs and legacy records created before tenant placement existed. Real M1 deployments assign this before the first Provision job is enqueued.

image_id.description: Boot image. As of Slice F images are multi-scope; the instance-create handler enforces that the principal can see this image (via the [`crate`]-level visibility predicate). Cross-scope references that the principal cannot see surface as `NotFound`.

primary_subnet_id.description: Subnet the instance's primary NIC attaches to. Phase 0 auto-creates a NIC at provisioning time; a future slice adds explicit NIC records that operators can manage separately. Subnet must live in a VPC inside this project.

ssh_key_ids.description: SSH keys to inject into the instance's authorized_keys at first boot. Each key id must live in the same silo as the instance. May be empty (no key injection).

InstanceBrand.description:
SmartOS brand the instance's host-side VM runs as.

Derived at create time from the boot [`Image`]'s [`ImageCompatibility::brand`] (the only place tritond currently learns it). `NotApplicable` is the default — it covers both records created before this field existed and images registered via the explicit-fields path that carries no compatibility block.

Used by the console surface (VNC framebuffer is only meaningful for `Bhyve` / `Kvm`) and by the UI to label instances. It is `#[serde(other)]` on `NotApplicable` so an unrecognised future brand string round-trips harmlessly rather than failing deserialization.

oneOf.description: `kvm` HVM zone (legacy hypervisor; serial + VNC consoles).
`bhyve` HVM zone (serial via the zone console; VNC framebuffer).
`lx` Linux-syscall zone (serial via the zone console only).
`joyent-minimal` native SmartOS zone (serial via the zone console only).
Brand not recorded / not recognised. The console surface treats this as "serial only".

JobKind.description:
What a control-plane job asks an agent to do.

Instance variants carry a target `instance_id` so an agent can look up VM state and drive the lifecycle forward. Edge variants carry an `edge_instance_id`; `manifest_bytes` is the exact fhrun manifest payload the target CN should apply.

This enum is `#[non_exhaustive]` so adding post-v1 variants (for example Migrate, Resize, or AF_XDP-specific edge work) is not a breaking change for downstream matchers.

oneOf.description: Drive a Pending instance through Provisioning → Running. Used both for first-time create and for `start` (which transitions Stopped → Pending and then enqueues a Provision).
Drive a Running instance through Stopping → Stopped.
Drive a Running instance through Stopping → Pending → Provisioning → Running. The agent is responsible for the whole cycle; the operator never sees Pending in between.

Best-effort `vmadm delete` follow-up, enqueued by the `instance_delete` handler *after* the tritond record is already cleared. The agent's vmadm-delete must be idempotent — on a host where the zone never existed (e.g. agent crashed before its prior Provision), the agent reports `Completed` rather than `Failed`. v0 trade-off: if the agent is unreachable, the zone leaks until an operator runs `vmadm delete` manually. A future slice promotes this to a Deleting → Deleted lifecycle gated on agent ack.

Apply or update a firehyve/fhrun edge instance on the target CN. The manifest is JSON bytes rendered by tritond from the EdgeCluster's desired state; the agent persists it to the host runtime path, asks fhrun/firehyve to converge, and reports realization for the carried EdgeCluster generation.
Reap a firehyve/fhrun edge instance that no longer has a desired EdgeCluster binding.

JobOutcome.description:
Outcome a worker reports when finishing a job.

Wire-stable: rides as the body of `POST /v2/agent/jobs/{id}/complete` when a real `tritonagent` is reporting back. The serde tag is `kind` and the case is snake_case — matches every other tagged enum on the wire (e.g. [`JobStatus`]).

JobStatus.description:
Lifecycle of a single provisioning job.

`Pending` → claimable by the next agent that polls. `InProgress` → an agent has claimed it; the agent is responsible for driving to `Completed` or `Failed`. Terminal states (`Completed`, `Failed`) are not re-queued automatically — operators retry by issuing the originating action again.

LegacyCnSummary.description:
Per-CN summary returned by `GET /v2/admin/legacy/cns`.

Distinct from [`CnView`]: this view rolls up the discovery classifier's per-CN counts (how many tritond-managed instances vs unmanaged legacy zones) so a fleet-admin operator can spot CNs that still have legacy zones to adopt.

legacy_vm_count.description: Count of legacy (unmanaged) zones tritond's classifier has observed on this CN.

managed_instance_count.description: Count of tritond-managed instances currently placed on this CN.

LegacyNic.description: Per-NIC layout for a [`LegacyVm`]. Distinct from [`Nic`] (the tritond-managed NIC type) because legacy zones may carry non-tritond NIC tags (`admin`, `external`, customer VLAN tags), and the IP may have come from DHCP rather than a tritond subnet allocation. Mirrors the per-NIC fields the Phase D adoption flow's pre-flight needs (IP-collision check, network-rewrite preview).

LegacyVm.description:
A SmartOS zone observed on a registered CN that does not carry the tritond `internal_metadata` identity tags -- i.e. it pre-existed before tritonagent was installed on the CN, or was created by an operator running `vmadm create` directly.

Legacy VMs live in their own FDB keyspace (`legacy_vm/...`) and are visible only to fleet-admin operators via `/v2/admin/legacy/vms`. They are NOT part of any tenant's workload tree until adopted (deferred Phase D).

alias.description: Operator-assigned zone alias (the human-readable name shown by `vmadm list`). Used as the row name in the admin UI.

host_cn_uuid.description: CN currently hosting the zone. Updated by the classifier when the same `smartos_uuid` reports from a different CN (caused by an out-of-band `vmadm send|recv` evacuation).

legacy_owner_uuid.description: `vmadm`'s `owner_uuid` field (the legacy SmartOS owner). Distinct from any tritond identity. May be the zero UUID for system zones.

smartos_uuid.description: SmartOS zone uuid. Stable across status reports.

LifecycleState.description:
Lifecycle state of a tenant instance. The state machine:

```text create ↓ Pending ──→ Provisioning ──┬→ Running ←──┬── Stopped │             │      ↑ │             │      │ ↓             ↓      │ Failed        Stopping─┘ ```

Phase 0 collapses Pending → Provisioning → Running into a synchronous transition inside the create handler (there is no agent yet). The full async path lands when the provisioning intent queue + stub executor slice ships.

Operator-driven transitions: start (Stopped → Pending), stop (Running → Stopping), restart (Running → Stopping → Pending). Agent-driven transitions: Pending → Provisioning → Running, Provisioning → Failed, Stopping → Stopped.

Delete is allowed only from Stopped or Failed; deleting a Running instance returns 409.

oneOf.description: Newly created; not yet picked up by an agent.
An agent has claimed the provisioning job and is working.
Up and running.
Stop requested; agent is winding down.
Fully stopped; safe to delete or restart.
Unrecoverable error. Inspect `reason`; the instance must be deleted (no in-place recovery in Phase 0).

LogBatch.description: Wire shape for the agent's ingest call. Posted in small batches (one batch per source per tick); tritond rejects batches larger than [`LogBatch::MAX_LINES`] to bound memory.

lines.description: The lines themselves, in chronological (file) order.

source.description: Which file on the zone these lines came from.

truncated_before.description: Whether the agent had to skip bytes between the previous batch and the first line here (e.g. because the file grew faster than the cap-per-tick limit). When `true` the UI shows a "... N bytes skipped ..." marker so operators know the stream isn't lossless.

LogLine.description:
One line from a log file, as the agent decodes it.

Timestamps are best-effort: the platform log uses syslog-style `bunyan` records with a parseable `time` field; the console log usually does not. When timestamp parsing fails the agent stamps `ingest_ts` instead so the UI can still order lines monotonically.

ingest_ts.description: When the agent observed the line. Always present; used as a monotonic ordering key when `ts` is `None`.

level.description: Best-effort severity. `info` / `warn` / `error` / `debug` when the agent could detect it (e.g. from bunyan `level`). `None` for raw text.

text.description: The line itself, with the trailing newline stripped. May be empty (blank line in the source).

ts.description: Best-effort wall-clock timestamp from the line itself. `None` means the line had no parseable timestamp -- consumers should fall through to `ingest_ts` for ordering.

LogSource.description: Source file the line came from. Stable on the wire -- new sources (e.g. SDC's `metadata.log`) get new variants rather than re-purposing existing ones. `#[non_exhaustive]` so adding a variant is non-breaking for clients.

oneOf.description: `/zones/<uuid>/logs/console.log` -- guest console output.
`/zones/<uuid>/logs/platform.log` -- SmartOS platform log.

older_dropped.description: `true` when the ring buffer dropped older lines before the oldest line returned (i.e. there's more history available in a follow-up backend like ClickHouse, when wired).

LoginRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v2/auth/login`.

`password` is a [`RedactedString`] so a stray `Debug` of this struct does not print the credential and so the in-memory copy is zeroed when the value drops.

ManagedIdentity.description:
Tamper-evident identity for a tritond-managed zone.

Minted by tritond at blueprint-fetch time using the per-deployment HMAC key (see `tritond_auth::IdentityHmacKey`). Tritonagent stamps the four fields verbatim into the zone's SmartOS `internal_metadata` (the four `TRITOND_METADATA_*` keys above) inside the same `vmadm create` payload that brings the zone into existence -- so a status report cannot fire between zone creation and identity write.

On a later status report, the classifier reads the four fields out of the report's `internal_metadata`, recomputes the HMAC from the reported triple, and compares constant-time. A mismatch (or missing tag) means the metadata was tampered with in-zone via `mdata-put` or copied from another deployment, and the zone is quarantined as `StaleFingerprint` rather than treated as managed.

identity_hmac.description: Lowercase hex of HMAC-SHA256 over the canonical `(instance_id, tenant_id, project_id)` triple. Signed with the per-deployment `IdentityHmac` system key.

instance_id.description: Tritond `Instance.id`. Reused as the SmartOS zone UUID at `vmadm create` time.

MetaEntry.description: One metadata entry as it appears on the wire (list / get responses). `value` is JSON; the flags + audit fields are flat at the top level so the OpenAPI schema reads cleanly.

guest_visible.description: Visible to processes inside the VM via IMDS. See [`default_guest_visible`] for the per-scope/per-prefix default. Generalizes the legacy SmartOS `internal_metadata` notion (`guest_visible == false` at instance scope).

guest_writable.description: The in-VM IMDS `PUT` may write this key. Only ever true for `guest/*` keys at instance scope on a writeback-enabled instance.

updated_by.description: Who last wrote it: a user UUID, `"guest:<instance-id>"` for an in-VM `PUT`, or `"system"` for seed values.

value.description: The stored value. Capped at [`MAX_META_VALUE_BYTES`] when serialized.

NatGateway.description:
Project-owned VPC egress point. A NAT gateway reserves one public address from the same Phase 0 public pool used by [`FloatingIp`], then downstream edge realization decides where and how that address is programmed.

The stored record carries `desired_generation`; [`Self::realized`] is computed from the realization rows at read time.

desired_generation.description: Monotonic desired-state generation. Create starts at 1; future wire-affecting mutations increment it atomically.

edge_cluster_id.description: Edge cluster selected to host this NAT gateway. `None` until edge placement lands in the Agent D/E slices.

family.description: Public address family requested at create time.

public_address.description: Public source address reserved for egress.

realized.description: Read-time projection of the per-realizer rows for this NAT gateway. This is not stored as a denormalized field.

NetworkRealizationRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v2/agent/network-realization`.

Agents report one `(resource, realizer)` row at a time. Tritond validates the resource exists and then lets the store enforce monotonic generation reporting for that tuple.

NetworkResourceId.description:
Tagged identity of a network resource that may have realization rows. The realization endpoint (Slice H-13) dispatches into the matching record by `(kind, id)`. v1 ships variants for every resource the design doc names (§6); the `#[non_exhaustive]` posture allows post-v1 additions.

Wire shape: `{ "kind": "nat_gateway", "id": "<uuid>" }`.

oneOf.description: A `Vpc`. Populated after H-4 widens `Vpc` with `desired_generation`.
A `Subnet`. Populated after H-4 widens `Subnet` with `desired_generation`.
A `RouteTable`. Populated when H-5 lands.
A `Route`. Populated when H-6 lands.
A `SecurityGroup`. Populated when H-7 lands.
A `SecurityGroupRule`. Populated when H-8 lands.
A NIC↔SG attachment. Populated when H-9 lands.
A `NatGateway`. Populated when H-2 lands. This is the first realized resource the codebase will exercise end-to-end (intent + realization) in the H-1..H-3 cluster.
A `FloatingIp`. Populated when H-11 widens the existing `FloatingIp` struct with `desired_generation` + termination fields.
An `EdgeCluster` hosting system-owned edge dataplane work.

NewApiKey.description:
Request body for `POST /v2/auth/api-keys`.

`scope` defaults to [`ApiKeyScope::Full`] when omitted on the wire — preserves the pre-scope behaviour where every minted key has the full permissions of the owning user. Operators who want a least-privilege key (e.g. for a CI pipeline that only reads audit logs) pass `scope: "read_only"` or `scope: "audit_only"` at create time.

NewFirewallRule.description: Request body for creating a [`FirewallRule`]. The owning tenant / project / VPC come from the URL path; the server assigns `id` and `created_at`.

NewFloatingIp.description: Request body for allocating a new FloatingIp. The server picks the actual address from the family-specific Phase 0 pool; the caller asks for `V4` or `V6` and gets the lowest free address.

NewIdpConfig.description: Request body for `POST /v2/tenants/{tenant_id}/idp`. tritond **eagerly** fetches the IdP's discovery document on this call; a 4xx/5xx return means the IdP isn't reachable or doesn't speak OIDC, and the config is not persisted.

audience.description: Expected `aud` claim. Defaults to `client_id` when omitted.

NewImage.description: Request body for registering an image in any scope's catalog. The owning scope (Public / Silo / Tenant / Project / User) is inferred from the URL path the request hit, *not* from the body. The server assigns `id` and `created_at`.

compatibility.description: Optional host-compatibility constraints. Populated by the server when an image is registered via the bundle ingest path; absent when the operator passed individual fields by hand. The per-CN agent enforces these when `Some`.

id.description: Optional UUID to pin for the new image. When `None` (the usual case), the server derives the UUID deterministically from `(scope, sha256)` via [`derive_image_id`] — same content in the same scope always yields the same id across hosts and replays, so the per-CN agent's content-addressed ZFS dataset (`zones/<image_id>`) collapses identical bytes into one import. Different scopes registering the same content yield distinct ids (no cross-scope collisions). `Some(...)` is only useful for cross-cluster mirroring scenarios where the operator wants tritond's id to match a UUID minted elsewhere; the store rejects the create with [`StoreError::Conflict`] if the id is already in use.

NewImageFromBundle.description:
Request body for `POST /v2/silos/{silo_id}/image-bundles`. (Bundle ingest stays silo-scoped through slice F; multi-scope bundle ingest is a future concern.)

`bundle_url` points at a tritond image bundle (an uncompressed tar with `manifest.json` + `content.zfs.gz`, produced by the `tritonimg-build` CLI). tritond fetches the bundle once at registration time, validates the manifest, re-hashes the content against the manifest's claimed sha256, and populates the Image record's `name`, `version`, `os`, `size_bytes`, `sha256`, and `compatibility` from the manifest. Operators don't pass any of those fields by hand — the bundle is the source of truth.

The bundle URL is also recorded as the Image's `source_url` so the per-CN agent fetches the same bundle at provision time (extracts manifest, sha256-verifies content, ZFS- receives).

NewInstance.description: Request body for creating an instance. The owning tenant + project come from the URL path. The server assigns `id`, initial `lifecycle`, `created_at`, and `updated_at`.

extra_nics.description: Additional NICs beyond the primary one on `primary_subnet_id`. Each entry causes the store to allocate one NIC + IP from the named subnet at create time. The `InstanceCreateResult.nics` Vec returns all of them in declaration order (primary at index 0). The agent's vmadm payload iterates over the Vec and attaches `net0`, `net1`, …

mac.description: Optional MAC address to pin on the primary NIC. Accepted in any case + any of the usual separator styles (`02:08:20:ab:cd:ef`, `02-08-20-AB-CD-EF`, `0208.20ab.cdef`). Used to opt into sticky-by-MAC IPAM (γ.2): if a reservation or prior lease in the parent VPC matches this MAC and that address is free, the instance is allocated that IP. None (the default) keeps the legacy auto-generated MAC behaviour.

NewInstanceNic.description: One additional NIC requested at instance create time. A future slice will let operators attach more after create via a dedicated POST endpoint; for v0 the only way to add a non-primary NIC is to declare it here.

name.description: Operator-friendly NIC label (`primary`, `db-tier`, …). Must be unique within the instance.

subnet_id.description: Subnet to draw this NIC's IP from. Must live in a VPC inside the same project as the instance.

NewNatGateway.description: Request body for creating a [`NatGateway`]. Parentage is inferred from the tenant/project/VPC URL path. The server assigns the id, public address, generation, and timestamps.

NewProject.description: Request body for creating a project. The owning tenant comes from the URL path, not the body.

NewQuota.description: Request body for setting a project's quota. The owning tenant and project come from the URL path. The server assigns `updated_at`.

NewRoute.description: Request body for creating a route inside a route table.

destination.description: Destination CIDR. Wire format is the canonical CIDR string.

NewRouteTable.description: Request body for creating an additional route table in a VPC. Parentage is inferred from the URL path once the public API lands.

NewSilo.description:
Request body for creating a silo.

Distinct from [`Silo`] because the server assigns `id` and `created_at`. `description` is optional on the wire and stored as an empty string when omitted.

NewSshKey.description: Request body for registering a new SSH key in any scope's catalog. The owning scope (Public / Silo / Tenant / Project / User) is inferred from the URL path the request hit, *not* from the body. The server assigns `id`, `fingerprint`, and `created_at`.

NewSubnet.description: Request body for creating a subnet. The owning tenant, project, and VPC come from the URL path. The server assigns `id` and `created_at`. At least one of `ipv4_block` / `ipv6_block` must be `Some`; the API rejects requests with both `None`.

NewTenant.description: Request body for creating a tenant. The owning silo comes from the URL path, not the body.

NewVpc.description: Request body for creating a VPC. The owning tenant + project come from the URL path, not the body. The server assigns `id`, `vni`, and `created_at`. At least one of `ipv4_block` / `ipv6_block` must be `Some`; the API rejects requests with both `None`.

Nic.description:
Per-instance network interface. Auto-created at instance create time with a single "primary" NIC; multi-NIC attach/detach is a future slice. Mirrors what the dataplane (OPTE per-NIC config) will eventually consume — guest MAC, primary IPv4/IPv6 in the subnet's CIDR.

Phase 0 stores only the per-NIC fields the agent needs to open the overlay attachment for an instance:

* `mac` — guest MAC address. Locally-administered (`02:` prefix) + 5 random bytes; uniqueness is rack-wide. * `primary_ipv4` / `primary_ipv6` — addresses allocated from the parent subnet's CIDR. Each family is `Some` only if the subnet has a CIDR for that family.

External IPs, attached_subnets (other-subnet routing), and firewall rules are *not* on the NIC record — they live on future External-IP / Route / FirewallRule resources.

mac.description: Guest MAC, formatted as 6 lowercase hex bytes separated by colons (e.g. `02:1a:b3:cd:ef:42`).

primary_ipv4.description: Primary IPv4 from `subnet.ipv4_block`. `None` if the parent subnet is IPv6-only.

primary_ipv6.description: Primary IPv6 from `subnet.ipv6_block`. `None` if the parent subnet is IPv4-only.

OpenAutoApproveRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /v2/cns/auto-approve`.

Opens (or replaces) the global auto-approve window. Bounded by both wall-time and a remaining-count budget so a forgotten window can't stay open forever; tritond clamps `duration_secs` to the 24h hard cap server-side.

count.description: Maximum number of registrations to auto-approve before the window closes early. `None` means "time-bound only".

Outcome.description: Outcome of the action after the handler finished.

oneOf.description: Handler completed successfully. `resource` carries the affected entity uid when known (e.g. `Silo::"<uuid>"`).
Cedar denied; handler did not run.
Authentication failed (bad password, expired token, missing key).
Caller-side error (4xx other than 401/403).
Server-side error (5xx).

PresignGetRequest.description: Body of `POST /v2/storage/clusters/{id}/s3/presign/get`. Same shape as the PUT request — kept as a distinct type for audit and API-doc clarity.

PresignResponse.description: Response shared by `presign/put` and `presign/get`. The browser uses these fields verbatim — the URL is fully signed; `method` tells the client which verb to issue; `headers` is reserved for future use when we sign headers beyond `host` (today empty).

headers.description: Headers the client must send verbatim. Empty in the current signer scope; reserved for future Content-Type / x-amz-* signing.

method.description: HTTP verb the URL is signed for (`"PUT"` or `"GET"`).

Project.description:
Sub-tenancy boundary inside a tenant. Workload resources (instances, volumes, networks) nest under projects.

E-3 re-parented projects from `silo_id` to `tenant_id`: the silo brand-level container owns one or more tenants; tenants own projects; projects own workloads. The owning silo, if needed, is derivable via [`Tenant::silo_id`].

ProvisioningBlueprint.description:
Materialised view of everything the agent needs to act on a claimed [`ProvisioningJob`]. Returned by `GET /v2/agent/jobs/{job_id}/blueprint`.

The shape is intentionally a flat bundle: instance + image + nics + subnets + disks + ssh public keys, all in one response. That lets the agent issue exactly one round-trip per claimed job, and keeps the queue payload itself opaque to the agent's needs (a Provision job will eventually want different fields than a hypothetical Migrate or Resize, and embedding everything in the [`ProvisioningJob`] would force lockstep schema migration).

Tritond's `Instance::id` is the canonical identity reused downstream: the agent passes it as the SmartOS zone UUID at `vmadm create`, so subsequent Stop/Restart jobs can address the zone by the same id without a separate mapping table.

Optional fields reflect the per-`JobKind` shape:

* `Provision` — `instance`, `image`, `nics`, `disks`, and any `ssh_public_keys` are populated. `subnets` carries the referenced subnet records so the agent can derive static guest network metadata without relying on dataplane DHCP. The agent has everything it needs to call `vmadm create`. * `Stop` / `Restart` — `instance` populated, others may be empty. The agent only needs `instance.id` to call `vmadm stop` / `vmadm reboot`.

disks.description: All disks attached to the instance. Empty for non-Provision jobs.

image.description: Boot image record. Populated for Provision jobs; absent for Stop/Restart.

instance.description: `Some(...)` when the underlying instance still exists; `None` if a concurrent operator delete raced the agent's claim. The agent must report `Failed` for this case rather than acting on a phantom instance.

managed_identity.description: Tamper-evident managed-zone identity. `Some` for `Provision` jobs (tritond mints + signs at blueprint-fetch time using the per-deployment HMAC key); `None` for `Stop` / `Restart` / `Delete` (the zone identity is already on disk from its original provision) and for any non-instance kind. The agent stamps these four fields verbatim into the zone's `internal_metadata` inside the `vmadm create` payload.

nics.description: All NICs attached to the instance. Empty for non-Provision jobs.

ssh_public_keys.description: Raw openssh-form public keys to inject via the SmartOS `root_authorized_keys` metadata at zone-create time. Resolved from `Instance::ssh_key_ids`.

subnets.description: Subnets referenced by `nics`, deduplicated by id. Empty for non-Provision jobs.

ProvisioningJob.description:
A unit of work for a provisioning agent. Created by the tritond instance handlers; consumed by an agent (the in-process stub today; a real `tritonagent` per CN in the future).

The wire shape is stable across Phase 0 and the eventual real agent — the only thing that changes is *who* claims and completes jobs.

claimed_at.description: Set when the job was first claimed by an agent.

claimed_by.description: Identifier of the agent that claimed the job. In Phase 0 this is the in-process stub's name (e.g. `"stub-provisioner"`).

completed_at.description: Set when the job reached a terminal status (Completed or Failed).

seq.description: Monotonically-increasing sequence number that determines the queue order. Older jobs (lower seq) are claimed first. Server-assigned at enqueue time.

target_cn_uuid.description: Optional placement: when `Some(server_uuid)`, only an agent bound to that CN will claim the job. When `None`, any claimer (the in-process stub or any bound agent) can claim. Tenant instance handlers populate this from [`Instance::host_cn_uuid`] once placement has selected a SmartOS host; tests and legacy records may remain unrouted.

Quota.description:
Per-project resource quota. Singleton: each project has at most one quota record. Set with PUT, read with GET, remove with DELETE (project becomes "unlimited" — no record means no enforcement).

Enforcement is *not* shipped in Phase 0 — these limits are stored and readable but no instance-create flow consults them yet. The shape is fixed now so the enforcement layer (Tier 3) has a stable contract to build against.

Limits are absolute caps, not reservations: `cpu_limit = 8` means "this project may have up to 8 vCPUs across all running instances." Storage and memory are bytes; cpu and instance counts are simple `u32`.

cpu_limit.description: Maximum total vCPUs across all instances in the project.

disk_bytes.description: Maximum total disk across all instances + volumes in the project, in bytes.

instance_limit.description: Maximum number of running instances in the project.

memory_bytes.description: Maximum total memory across all instances in the project, in bytes.

RangeResult.description: Result of a range query. One entry per distinct series label seen in the window (e.g. `user`, `system`, `iowait`).

Realization.description: Per-realizer realization row. One per `(resource, realizer)` tuple; written by [`crate::Store::record_network_realization`] and read back by [`crate::Store::list_network_realizations`].

generation.description: Generation the realizer reports for the resource. Monotonic per `(resource, realizer)`: a write with `generation < existing.generation` is rejected with [`crate::StoreError::Conflict`] (the "backward report" case the Agent C contract calls out).

last_reported_at.description: Wall-clock time the row was last upserted by the realizer.

message.description: Free-form short diagnostic from the realizer. Surfaced verbatim in `tcadm net realized`. Kept short — detailed stderr belongs in agent logs and future support bundles, not in unbounded control-plane rows.

realizer.description: Which CN or edge cluster reported this row.

RealizationStatus.description: Outcome a realizer reports at a given generation. The variant set is deliberately small in v1: `Accepted` for "agent received the blueprint and handed it to its dataplane" (Agent B's `accepted_generation`); `Applied` for "dataplane confirmed the generation active" (the canonical terminal report); `Failed` for terminal apply failure with a short message. The enum is `#[non_exhaustive]` so post-v1 additions (e.g. `Compiling`, `Pending`) can land without breaking downstream matches.

oneOf.description: The realizer accepted the work and handed the blueprint to its backing dataplane. Aligns with Agent B's `accepted_generation` concept (see `proteus/docs/tritond-integration-v1.md`).
The dataplane confirmed the generation active (e.g. Proteus `GetGenerationStatus` returned `applied_generation >= generation` on the realizer's port).
The realizer failed to converge to this generation. The associated message should describe the phase (image fetch, blueprint apply, port start, ...).

RealizedNetworkState.description:
